west-bengal.name: West Bengal
west-bengal.description: West Bengal, located in eastern India, is a state rich in culture, literature, and natural beauty. From the bustling city of Kolkata to the serene hills of Darjeeling, the mangrove forests of Sundarbans, and the beach town of Digha, West Bengal offers incredible diversity for travelers.

cities.name: Kolkata
cities.description: Kolkata (formerly Calcutta), the City of Joy, is the cultural capital of India. Known for its colonial architecture, Howrah Bridge, Victoria Memorial, and vibrant street food culture. The city is a major business hub and gateway to eastern India.
cities.airport: Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose International Airport

cities.name: Siliguri
cities.description: Siliguri (also written as Shiliguri) is the gateway to Northeast India and the hills of Darjeeling. This bustling city serves as a major transit point for travelers heading to Darjeeling, Gangtok, and the Dooars region.

cities.name: Darjeeling
cities.description: Darjeeling (Darjiling), the Queen of the Hills, is world-famous for its tea gardens, the Darjeeling Himalayan Railway (Toy Train), and stunning views of Mount Kanchenjunga. A must-visit hill station in eastern India.

cities.name: Durgapur
cities.description: Durgapur is one of the most important industrial cities in West Bengal, known as the Steel City. It houses major industrial units and is a growing commercial center.

cities.name: Asansol
cities.description: Asansol is the second-largest city in West Bengal and an important industrial hub. Known for its coal mines and steel plants, it serves as a major railway junction.

cities.name: Howrah
cities.description: Howrah (Haora), the twin city of Kolkata across the Hooghly River, is home to the iconic Howrah Bridge and Howrah Junction — one of the busiest railway stations in India.

cities.name: Kharagpur
cities.description: Kharagpur is home to the prestigious Indian Institute of Technology (IIT Kharagpur) and has the longest railway platform in India.

cities.name: Bardhaman
cities.description: Bardhaman (Burdwan/Barddhaman) is a historic city known for its rice cultivation and cultural heritage. The city has several temples and historical monuments.

cities.name: Malda
cities.description: Malda is a historically significant district, home to the ancient ruins of Gaur and Pandua — the medieval capitals of Bengal. The region is famous for its mangoes.

cities.name: Murshidabad
cities.description: Murshidabad was the former capital of Bengal during the Nawab era. The district is rich in Mughal architecture and historical significance.

cities.name: Cooch Behar
cities.description: Cooch Behar is a historically significant town famous for the stunning Cooch Behar Palace, modeled after Buckingham Palace. The region offers a unique blend of culture and natural beauty.

cities.name: Jalpaiguri
cities.description: Jalpaiguri is the gateway to Dooars, the door to Bhutan and Northeast India. Known for its lush tea gardens and proximity to Gorumara and Jaldapara National Parks.

cities.name: Bankura
cities.description: Bankura is known for its terracotta art, Bankura horse, and Bishnupur temple town. The district is a treasure of Bengal's artistic and cultural heritage.

cities.name: Purulia
cities.description: Purulia is known for its tribal culture, Chhau dance, and scenic beauty. The Ajodhya Hills and Baranti Dam are popular attractions for nature lovers and trekkers.

cities.name: Midnapore
cities.description: Midnapore is a historic town with significant importance in India's freedom struggle. It serves as the district headquarters and has several cultural landmarks.

cities.name: Digha
cities.description: Digha is the most popular beach resort town in West Bengal. Known for its wide, sandy beach and calm sea waters, it's a favorite weekend getaway from Kolkata.

cities.name: Sundarbans
cities.description: The Sundarbans is the largest mangrove forest in the world and a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Home to the Royal Bengal Tiger, it offers unique boat safari experiences.

cities.name: Bishnupur
cities.description: Bishnupur is famous for its terracotta temples dating back to the 17th century. The Malla kings built these beautiful temples that showcase the region's artistic brilliance.

cities.name: Bolpur Shantiniketan
cities.description: Shantiniketan, founded by Rabindranath Tagore, is a cultural hub and home to Visva-Bharati University. The town celebrates art, music, and Tagore's legacy year-round.

cities.name: Dooars
cities.description: The Dooars region is a gateway to Bhutan and the northeastern states. Known for its dense forests, tea gardens, and wildlife sanctuaries, it offers incredible eco-tourism.

cities.name: Mandarmani
cities.description: Mandarmani is a popular seaside resort town in West Bengal, known for having the longest motorable beach in India. A favorite weekend getaway from Kolkata with beachside resorts and red crab sightings.

cities.name: Bakkhali
cities.description: Bakkhali is a serene sea beach town in South 24 Parganas, known for its calm and peaceful ambiance. The nearby Frasergunj and Henry Island are popular for boat rides and bird watching.

cities.name: Gangasagar
cities.description: Gangasagar (Sagar Island) is one of the holiest pilgrimage sites in India, where the River Ganga meets the Bay of Bengal. The annual Gangasagar Mela attracts millions of devotees for a holy dip.

cities.name: Mayapur
cities.description: Mayapur is the global headquarters of the International Society for Krishna Consciousness (ISKCON). The Temple of the Vedic Planetarium is one of the world's largest temples under construction.

cities.name: Nabadwip
cities.description: Nabadwip (Navadvipa) is a sacred town and the birthplace of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, the founder of the Gaudiya Vaishnavism movement. It is one of the most important pilgrimage centers in Bengal.

cities.name: Krishnanagar
cities.description: Krishnanagar is the administrative headquarters of Nadia district, famous for its traditional clay art (Ghurni art) and cultural heritage. The Krishnanagar Palace and Ghurni artist village are popular attractions.

cities.name: Diamond Harbour
cities.description: Diamond Harbour is a riverside town on the banks of the Hooghly River in South 24 Parganas. A popular day trip from Kolkata, it offers beautiful riverfront views, the historic Diamond Harbour Fort ruins, and fresh seafood.

cities.name: Haldia
cities.description: Haldia is a major port city and industrial hub in Purba Medinipur district. It is one of the most important commercial centers in West Bengal with petrochemical industries and the Haldia Dock Complex.

cities.name: Contai
cities.description: Contai (Kanthi) is a coastal city in Purba Medinipur district, serving as the gateway to popular beach destinations like Digha and Mandarmani. Known for its agriculture, fishing industry, and cultural heritage.

cities.name: Tamluk
cities.description: Tamluk (ancient Tamralipta) is one of the oldest continuously inhabited cities in Bengal. It was a major port of the ancient Mauryan and Gupta empires, and the district headquarters of Purba Medinipur.

cities.name: Alipurduar
cities.description: Alipurduar is the gateway to Bhutan and the Dooars region. It is the administrative headquarters of the Alipurduar district and surrounded by dense forests, tea gardens, and wildlife sanctuaries.

cities.name: Raiganj
cities.description: Raiganj is the administrative headquarters of Uttar Dinajpur district. It is home to the Raiganj Wildlife Sanctuary (Kulik Bird Sanctuary), one of the largest bird sanctuaries in Asia.

cities.name: Balurghat
cities.description: Balurghat is the administrative headquarters of Dakshin Dinajpur district, situated near the Bangladesh border. It is an important commercial and educational center in northern West Bengal.

cities.name: Baharampur
cities.description: Baharampur (Berhampore) is the administrative headquarters of Murshidabad district, situated on the banks of the Bhagirathi River. Known for its silk industry, historical significance, and proximity to Hazarduari Palace.

cities.name: Hooghly
cities.description: Hooghly (Chinsurah) is the headquarters of Hooghly district with a rich colonial history. It was a Dutch, Portuguese, and French colony. The district is also home to the famous Bandel Church and Imambara.

cities.name: Kolkata Airport
cities.description: Netaji Subhash Chandra Bose International Airport (CCU) is the primary airport serving the Kolkata metropolitan area.

cities.name: Salt Lake
cities.description: Salt Lake or Bidhannagar is a planned satellite town in the Indian state of West Bengal.

cities.name: New Town
cities.description: New Town is an emerging business and residential hub in the northeastern fringes of Kolkata.

cities.name: Satragachhi
cities.description: Satragachhi is an important locality in Howrah district, home to the Satragachhi railway station — a major junction serving as an alternative gateway to Howrah for passengers from South Bengal. Conveniently connected to Kolkata via Howrah Bridge.

cities.name: Uluberia
cities.description: Uluberia is a municipal town in Howrah district on the banks of the Hooghly River. Known for its jute mills, industrial units, and the Uluberia Railway Station. It serves as a transit point between Kolkata and Kharagpur.

cities.name: Serampore
cities.description: Serampore (Srirampur) is a historic town in Hooghly district, once a Danish colonial settlement. Known for Serampore College, one of the oldest universities in Asia, and the historic Frederiksnagore church. A popular day trip from Kolkata.

cities.name: Chandannagar
cities.description: Chandannagar (Chandernagore) is a historic French colonial town on the banks of the Hooghly River in Hooghly district. Famous for its Jagaddhatri Puja, the promenade (Strand), and the French architecture preserved from colonial times.

cities.name: Tarkeshwar
cities.description: Tarkeshwar (Tarakeswar) is a famous pilgrimage town in Hooghly district, home to the ancient Baba Tarkeshwar Shiva Temple — one of the most sacred Shiva temples in West Bengal. Lakhs of devotees visit during Shivratri and Shravan month.

cities.name: Kolaghat
cities.description: Kolaghat is an important junction town in East Medinipur, situated at the confluence of NH-16 and NH-116. It serves as a major transit point on the route to Digha, Haldia, and Mandarmani from Kolkata.

cities.name: Bagnan
cities.description: Bagnan is a town in Howrah district, situated on NH-6. It serves as an important junction for travelers heading towards Kharagpur and South Bengal. The town is known for its rice and jute trade.

cities.name: Arambagh
cities.description: Arambagh is the sub-divisional headquarters of Arambagh subdivision in Hooghly district. Known for the nearby Kamarpukur — birthplace of Sri Ramakrishna Paramahamsa — making it an important pilgrimage circuit in Bengal.

cities.name: Barasat
cities.description: Barasat is the administrative headquarters of North 24 Parganas district, one of the most populated districts in India. Located just 20 km from Kolkata, it is a major commercial hub and a key junction on the highway to North Bengal and Bangladesh border.

cities.name: Kalyani
cities.description: Kalyani is a planned satellite town in Nadia district, famous for its medical hub — JNMCH (Jawaharlal Nehru Memorial Hospital) and Kalyani University. A major destination for medical treatment from across West Bengal and Bangladesh.

cities.name: Bongaon
cities.description: Bongaon is a major city in North 24 Parganas near the Bangladesh border. It is an important trade and transit hub, serving as the entry point to the Petrapole-Benapole border crossing — India's busiest land port with Bangladesh.

cities.name: Ranaghat
cities.description: Ranaghat is an important city and railway junction in Nadia district, serving as a major transit point between Kolkata, Bongaon, Krishnanagar, and North Bengal. The city is known for its historical significance and cultural events.

cities.name: Basirhat
cities.description: Basirhat is the sub-divisional headquarters of Basirhat subdivision in North 24 Parganas, situated near the Bangladesh border and the Ichhamati River. An important commercial town serving the border region and Sundarbans.

cities.name: Barrackpore
cities.description: Barrackpore is a historic garrison town on the banks of the Hooghly River in North 24 Parganas, with deep roots in India's independence movement. Mangal Pandey, the hero of 1857, launched his revolt here. The Gandhi Ghat and Army Museum are major attractions.

cities.name: Dum Dum
cities.description: Dum Dum is a suburban area in North 24 Parganas, famous for being adjacent to Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose International Airport (Kolkata Airport). A major transit hub for air travelers, it also has significant industrial and residential zones.

cities.name: Habra
cities.description: Habra is a bustling commercial town in North 24 Parganas district. Located on the Barasat-Bongaon road, it serves as an important junction for travellers heading to the Sundarbans, Basirhat, and Bangladesh border areas.

cities.name: Naihati
cities.description: Naihati is a municipal town in North 24 Parganas on the west bank of the Hooghly River. Known for its jute mills, it is also the birthplace of renowned Bengali writer Bibhutibhushan Bandyopadhyay (author of Pather Panchali).

cities.name: Kakdwip
cities.description: Kakdwip is the main gateway to Gangasagar (Sagar Island) and a key town in South 24 Parganas. The Kachuberia ferry terminal here connects to Sagar Island. Kakdwip is also known for its fishing community and proximity to the Sundarbans.

cities.name: Jhargram
cities.description: Jhargram is the headquarters of Jhargram district, surrounded by dense sal forests and scenic countryside. Known as a forest retreat, it offers peace and natural beauty with the famous Jhargram Palace and proximity to tribal culture tourism.

cities.name: Raniganj
cities.description: Raniganj is a historic coal town in Paschim Bardhaman district, known as the birthplace of India's coal mining industry. The Raniganj Coalfield, established in 1774, is one of the largest coalfields in India.

cities.name: Kalna
cities.description: Kalna (Ambika Kalna) is a historic temple town in Purba Bardhaman district on the banks of the Hooghly River. Famous for the Kalna Rajbari complex with 108 Shiva temples arranged in two concentric circles — a unique architectural marvel of Bengal.

cities.name: Katwa
cities.description: Katwa is a historically significant town in Purba Bardhaman district on the confluence of the Bhagirathi and Ajay rivers. It was the birthplace of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's early follower Nityananda Prabhu and the site of the Battle of Katwa during Siraj ud-Daulah's time.

cities.name: Chakdah
cities.description: Chakdah is a town in Nadia district known for its agricultural produce, sports goods manufacturing, and as a transit point between Kolkata and Krishnanagar. The town is notable for producing many sportsmen and athletes.

cities.name: Islampur
cities.description: Islampur is an important city in Uttar Dinajpur district in North Bengal, serving as a major commercial hub for the region. Located on NH 31, it is a gateway to North Bengal, Sikkim, and Darjeeling from this route.

cities.name: Dalkhola
cities.description: Dalkhola is a strategic town in Uttar Dinajpur district at the trijunction of West Bengal, Bihar, and Jharkhand. It is a major transit point on NH 31 for travellers heading to North Bengal, Sikkim, and Northeast India.

cities.name: Lalbagh
cities.description: Lalbagh is a historical town in Murshidabad district, known for the ruins of the Lalbagh Fort — the incomplete palace of Nawab Shaista Khan and Aurangzeb's son. The town offers a glimpse into Bengal's Mughal-era history.

cities.name: Beldanga
cities.description: Beldanga is a town in Murshidabad district on the banks of the Bhagirathi River. Known for its silk weaving industry and as a railway junction connecting Baharampur to Kolkata and Azimganj.

cities.name: Karimpur
cities.description: Karimpur is a border town in Nadia district near the Bangladesh border. Known for vegetable farming and trade, it is one of the important agricultural towns of Nadia with regular commercial activity.

odisha.name: Odisha
odisha.description: Odisha (formerly Orissa) is a state on India's eastern coast known for its ancient temples, tribal cultures, and pristine beaches. From the Sun Temple at Konark to the sacred city of Puri and the wildlife sanctuaries of Simlipal, Odisha is a treasure trove for travelers.

cities.name: Bhubaneswar
cities.description: Bhubaneswar, the Temple City of India, is the capital of Odisha. With over 700 temples, it's a center of art, culture, and spirituality. The city is also a growing IT hub.

cities.name: Puri
cities.description: Puri is one of the four holy Dhams and home to the famous Jagannath Temple. The city's Golden Beach and the annual Rath Yatra festival attract millions of pilgrims and tourists.

cities.name: Cuttack
cities.description: Cuttack, the Silver City, is the former capital of Odisha and one of the oldest cities in India. Known for its silver filigree work, Barabati Fort, and Durga Puja celebrations.

cities.name: Rourkela
cities.description: Rourkela is a major industrial city, home to the Rourkela Steel Plant and NIT Rourkela. Located near beautiful waterfalls and the Mandira Dam.

cities.name: Sambalpur
cities.description: Sambalpur is known for its handloom textiles (Sambalpuri sarees), the famous Hirakud Dam, and the vibrant Sitalsasthi carnival.

cities.name: Berhampur
cities.description: Berhampur (Brahmapur) is a major commercial city in southern Odisha, known for its silk sarees and proximity to scenic beaches like Gopalpur-on-Sea.

cities.name: Balasore
cities.description: Balasore (Baleshwar) is famous for Chandipur Beach, where the sea recedes up to 5 km during low tide. It's also the site of India's missile testing range.

cities.name: Bhadrak
cities.description: Bhadrak is a district town in Odisha situated on the banks of the Salandi River. It is an important railway junction on the Howrah-Chennai main line and serves as a commercial hub for the surrounding coastal region. The Bhadrak Akhandalamani Temple is a popular religious site.

cities.name: Konark
cities.description: Konark is world-famous for the Sun Temple, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. The 13th-century temple is shaped like a giant chariot and is a masterpiece of Kalinga architecture.

cities.name: Chilika
cities.description: Chilika Lake is Asia's largest brackish water lagoon and a Ramsar wetland. It's a paradise for bird watchers, with millions of migratory birds visiting every winter.

cities.name: Dhenkanal
cities.description: Dhenkanal is a historic town known for its cultural heritage and proximity to Kapilash Temple. The district has lush forests and the Saptasajya hills.

cities.name: Koraput
cities.description: Koraput is known for its beautiful tribal culture, coffee plantations, and scenic landscapes. The region offers unique tribal tourism experiences.

cities.name: Rayagada
cities.description: Rayagada is known for the Dongariya Kondh tribe and the scenic Chatikona Valley. The region offers authentic tribal tourism experiences.

cities.name: Angul
cities.description: Angul is an important industrial city in Odisha, home to the NALCO aluminum smelter and thermal power plants. It is also known for the Satkosia Gorge wildlife sanctuary nearby.

cities.name: Jharsuguda
cities.description: Jharsuguda is an important industrial and mining city in western Odisha, known for its coal mines, power plants, and as a major railway junction connecting Odisha with Chhattisgarh.

cities.name: Baripada
cities.description: Baripada is the gateway to Simlipal National Park, one of India's major tiger reserves. The town is known for its tribal heritage, Chhau dance festival, and the famous Rath Yatra.

cities.name: Jeypore
cities.description: Jeypore (Koraput district) is known for its rich tribal culture, beautiful waterfalls, and as a gateway to the tribal hinterland of southern Odisha. The Haat Kali Temple and weekly tribal markets are major attractions.

cities.name: Gopalpur
cities.description: Gopalpur-on-Sea is a serene beach town in southern Odisha, known for its old-world charm, colonial-era lighthouse, and tranquil beach. Once a bustling port, it's now a peaceful beach getaway.

jharkhand.name: Jharkhand
jharkhand.description: Jharkhand, the Land of Forests, is blessed with stunning waterfalls, dense forests, and rich tribal culture. From the waterfalls around Ranchi to the religious significance of Deoghar and the industrial cities of Jamshedpur and Dhanbad, Jharkhand offers diverse experiences.

cities.name: Ranchi
cities.description: Ranchi, the capital of Jharkhand, is known as the City of Waterfalls. Surrounded by beautiful waterfalls like Hundru, Jonha, and Dassam Falls, Ranchi is a perfect blend of nature and urban development.

cities.name: Jamshedpur
cities.description: Jamshedpur, the Steel City, was founded by Tata Group and is India's first planned industrial city. Known for Jubilee Park, Dimna Lake, and the Tata Steel Works.

cities.name: Dhanbad
cities.description: Dhanbad is known as the Coal Capital of India. Home to IIT ISM and surrounded by numerous coal mines, it's a major industrial city in Jharkhand.

cities.name: Bokaro
cities.description: Bokaro Steel City is home to one of India's largest steel plants. The city is well-planned with beautiful parks and the Bokaro Steel Plant complex.

cities.name: Hazaribagh
cities.description: Hazaribagh, meaning 'City of a Thousand Gardens', is known for its natural beauty, Canary Hill, and the Hazaribagh Wildlife Sanctuary.

cities.name: Deoghar
cities.description: Deoghar is one of the most sacred cities in India, home to the Baidyanath Dham (one of the 12 Jyotirlingas). Millions of devotees visit during the Shravani Mela.

cities.name: Giridih
cities.description: Giridih is known for the Parasnath Hill, the most sacred place for Jainism. The hill has 24 Jain temples and is a popular pilgrimage destination.

cities.name: Ramgarh
cities.description: Ramgarh is known for its coal mines and scenic Rajrappa Temple located at the confluence of Damodar and Bhairavi rivers.

cities.name: Palamu
cities.description: Palamu is home to the Betla National Park (Palamu Tiger Reserve). The ancient Palamu Fort and dense forests make it a popular eco-tourism destination.

cities.name: Netarhat
cities.description: Netarhat is known as the 'Queen of Chotanagpur'. This hill station offers breathtaking sunrise and sunset views and is surrounded by dense sal forests.

cities.name: Betla
cities.description: Betla is famous for the Betla National Park, one of the first tiger reserves in India. The park is home to tigers, elephants, and diverse wildlife.

cities.name: Dumka
cities.description: Dumka is the sub-capital of Jharkhand and the administrative headquarters of Santal Pargana division. Known for its tribal heritage, Baskinath temple, and the picturesque Masanjore Dam.

cities.name: Chaibasa
cities.description: Chaibasa is the administrative headquarters of West Singhbhum district. Known for its tribal culture, iron ore mining, and proximity to beautiful waterfalls and dense forests of Saranda.

cities.name: Sahebganj
cities.description: Sahebganj is a historic town on the banks of the Ganges, known for the Rajmahal Hills, ancient fossils, and the beautiful Kanhaiya Sthan temple. It's an important river port town.

cities.name: Godda
cities.description: Godda is a quiet town in Jharkhand known for the Sundarban Dam and proximity to the Rajmahal Hills. The district is predominantly agricultural with rich tribal heritage.

cities.name: Latehar
cities.description: Latehar is the gateway to Netarhat hill station and home to the famous Lodh Falls, the highest waterfall in Jharkhand. The district is covered with dense forests and offers excellent trekking opportunities.

cities.name: Tatanagar
cities.description: Tatanagar is a major railway junction and a key gateway to Jamshedpur.

cities.name: Adityapur
cities.description: Adityapur is an industrial city adjacent to Jamshedpur in the Seraikela Kharsawan district.

cities.name: Khunti
cities.description: Khunti is the birthplace of Birsa Munda, the legendary tribal freedom fighter who led the Ulgulan revolt against British rule. Situated 35 km from Ranchi, the district is a pilgrimage for tribal heritage, dense forests, and beautiful waterfalls. The Birsa Munda Memorial Museum is a must-visit.

cities.name: Gumla
cities.description: Gumla is a scenic district town in Jharkhand surrounded by dense forests and beautiful waterfalls. Known for the ancient Nagpuri culture and tribal heritage, Gumla is home to the Nagpheni waterfall and the Anjandhaam Temple — a famous pilgrimage site for Hanuman devotees.

cities.name: Simdega
cities.description: Simdega is a tribal-dominated district in southwestern Jharkhand on the Odisha border. Known for its lush green forests, tribal culture, and the Keolajhar Forest, it is emerging as an eco-tourism destination. The district is known for producing many football players.

cities.name: Chatra
cities.description: Chatra is a picturesque district in Jharkhand known for beautiful waterfalls and scenic landscapes. The Tamas waterfall (also called Tandwa Falls) and the ancient Ituriaghat are popular attractions. Chatra sits on the old Grand Trunk Road route.

cities.name: Lohardaga
cities.description: Lohardaga is a district town in Jharkhand, 60 km from Ranchi, known for its rich bauxite deposits and tribal culture. The Koel River flows through the district, and it is a quiet, scenic town on the route to Netarhat hill station.

cities.name: Pakur
cities.description: Pakur is a district town in the Santhal Pargana division of Jharkhand on the West Bengal border. Known for stone quarrying and mining, it sits on the banks of the Brahmani River. The district has significant tribal Santhal population and cultural heritage.

cities.name: Jamtara
cities.description: Jamtara is a district town in Jharkhand, 150 km from Kolkata, known as an industrial transit zone near the WB-Jharkhand border. The town is surrounded by coalfields and is strategically located between Asansol and Dhanbad on the national highway.

cities.name: Koderma
cities.description: Koderma is a district town in Jharkhand known for its mica deposits — earning it the title 'Mica Capital of the World'. Located on NH 2 (Grand Trunk Road) between Hazaribagh and Gaya, it is an important transit point.

cities.name: Seraikela
cities.description: Seraikela is a historic town in Seraikela-Kharsawan district, famous for its royal heritage and the traditional Seraikela Chhau dance — one of the three major styles of the vibrant Chhau martial dance recognized by UNESCO. Located near Jamshedpur, it is an important cultural destination.

bihar.name: Bihar
bihar.description: Bihar, one of India's oldest civilizations, is the land of the Buddha and ancient empires. Home to sacred Buddhist circuit sites like Bodh Gaya and Nalanda, the holy pilgrim city of Gaya, and the historic capital Patna on the Ganga. A significant destination for spiritual, cultural, and heritage tourism from Kolkata.

cities.name: Patna
cities.description: Patna, the capital of Bihar and one of the oldest continuously inhabited cities in the world, sits on the banks of the Ganga. The ancient city of Pataliputra was the capital of the Maurya Empire. Today, Patna is a major pilgrimage and heritage tourism hub with a rich history spanning over 2,500 years.

cities.name: Gaya
cities.description: Gaya is one of the most sacred pilgrimage cities in India, revered by Hindus for Pitru Paksha rituals (Pind Daan) on the banks of the Phalgu River. The Vishnupad Temple is one of the most important Vaishnava shrines. Gaya is also the gateway to Bodh Gaya, making it a dual Hindu-Buddhist pilgrimage hub.

cities.name: Bodh Gaya
cities.description: Bodh Gaya is the most sacred site in Buddhism — where Siddhartha Gautama attained enlightenment under the Bodhi Tree. A UNESCO World Heritage Site, it attracts Buddhist pilgrims from across the world. The Mahabodhi Temple Complex is a masterpiece of Indian architecture.

cities.name: Nalanda
cities.description: Nalanda was home to the ancient Nalanda University, one of the greatest centers of learning in the ancient world (5th–12th century). A UNESCO World Heritage Site, the ruins of Nalanda attract historians, Buddhist pilgrims, and culture tourists from across the world.

cities.name: Rajgir
cities.description: Rajgir (Rajagriha), surrounded by five hills, is one of the most important Buddhist and Jain pilgrimage sites. It was the first capital of the Magadha Empire and a key place in Buddhist history. The Vishwa Shanti Stupa and ropeway are major attractions.

cities.name: Muzaffarpur
cities.description: Muzaffarpur is a major commercial city in North Bihar, famous for its Shahi litchi (Muzaffarpur litchi) and as an important transport hub. The city has historical significance with the Ramna Maidan and several temples.

uttar-pradesh.name: Uttar Pradesh
uttar-pradesh.description: Uttar Pradesh is India's most populous state and the spiritual heartland of the nation. Home to some of the holiest cities in Hinduism — Varanasi (Kashi), Prayagraj (Allahabad), Mathura, and Ayodhya — it attracts millions of pilgrims and tourists every year from Kolkata and across India.

cities.name: Varanasi
cities.description: Varanasi (Kashi/Banaras) is one of the oldest continuously inhabited cities in the world and the spiritual capital of India. The ghats on the Ganga, especially Dashashwamedh and Manikarnika, are iconic. The evening Ganga Aarti is a mesmerizing spiritual experience. A top pilgrimage and cultural destination for Bengalis.

cities.name: Prayagraj
cities.description: Prayagraj (Allahabad) is one of the holiest cities in Hinduism, situated at the Triveni Sangam — the confluence of the Ganga, Yamuna, and the mythical Saraswati rivers. The city hosts the Kumbh Mela, the world's largest religious gathering. A major destination for Bengali pilgrims.
